methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-driven AI education infrastructure in Richmond, demonstrating peer network potential beyond NoVA's corporate concentration—a test case for distributed deep capability.

signals.title: ODU and Google launch AI incubator to boost innovation in Hampton Roads - 13newsnow.com
signals.signal_reading: ODU-Google AI incubator represents institutional partnership replicating NoVA's model in Hampton Roads, but concentration in university hubs risks reinforcing metro-centric excellence rather than regional distribution.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding upskilling infrastructure signals anxiety about displacement rather than capability-building—shallow disruption without depth of preparation.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates distributed capacity-building in coal transition regions, potentially enabling peer networks that bypass traditional metro-to-rural knowledge flows.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses embracing AI through platform adoption indicates broad but shallow distribution—organizational use of turnkey tools rather than deep technical capability development or custom implementation.
